sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Matthew, has had lower back pain for a long time, which worsened about a month ago.
He reports that the pain radiates from the lower back, down the left leg, and affects the hip, side of the leg, side of the calf, and big toe.
The pain is described as an electrical toothache and is rated 6 out of 10 at the moment, but has been as high as 13 out of 10.
The patient reports that standing, walking, and activities such as coughing and sneezing exacerbate the pain, while laying down provides some relief.
He has been bedridden due to the pain and has been using a cane to walk.
He denies numbness or tingling in the lower extremities.
The patient has a history of a fall off a roof 30 years ago, which may have caused a fracture or disk herniation.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of a fall off a roof 30 years ago, which may have caused a fracture or disk herniation.
Review of systems: The patient reports no other symptoms in other organs.
Current medications: Not mentioned.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure, pulse rate, oxygen saturation, and temperature are within normal limits.
Physical exam: The patient has pain to palpation at the L4 vertebrae.
He has pain with flexion and extension of the back.
The patient has 4 out of 5 strength in the left leg and 5 out of 5 strength in the right leg.
Reflexes are brisk and motor and sensory are intact in both lower extremities.
Test Results: The MRI shows a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae associated with nerve root impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a diagnosis of acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement causing lower back pain and radiating pain down the left leg.
Problem list: Acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement; lower back pain; radiating pain down the left leg.
Plan:
I prescribed [NOT MENTIONED] [NOT MENTIONED] [NOT MENTIONED] [NOT MENTIONED].
I recommended an epidural steroid injection to be administered today to reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
The patient will be referred to physical therapy (PT) to strengthen the area.
The patient will see me again in about a month to assess the effectiveness of the injection.
Follow-up: The patient will see me again in about a month.
